onboarding.s1_intro: You've just been appointed CEO of {{team}}, a {{industry}} company headquartered in {{home_market}}.
onboarding.s1_mission: Your mission: take this company global. Expand into new markets, build technology platforms, compete against rivals, and earn the confidence of investors, governments, and customers across 5 regions — each with different cultures, regulations, currencies, and competitive dynamics.
onboarding.s1_origin: But going global isn't just about selling in more places. Your company carries its origin with it. Where you're from affects how customers trust you, how governments treat you, and how easily your talent operates abroad. Navigating these institutional barriers is the real challenge — and the real learning.
onboarding.s2_intro: Five forces will shape your success:
onboarding.s2_origin_trust: Customers and regulators in foreign markets may trust you less based on where you're headquartered. A company from {{home_market}} faces different barriers in each region. Trust is earned through sustained presence, local investment, compliance, and sometimes acquiring a local brand.
onboarding.s2_cultural_distance: Your talent is world-class at headquarters, but how effectively can they operate in Lagos, São Paulo, or Frankfurt? Allocating staff to foreign markets and investing in localization determines whether your capability translates into results.
onboarding.s2_tech_sovereignty: Build your technology in-house and you own it forever. License it and you move faster — but a trade restriction could degrade your platform overnight.
onboarding.s2_gov_policy: Each government has industrial priorities. Align with them and earn tax incentives, procurement contracts, and regulatory goodwill. Ignore them and face warnings, restrictions, or market access revocation.
onboarding.s2_competitive: AI competitors react to your moves. AI investors trade your shares based on your strategy. Alliance partners evaluate your commitment every round. Nothing stays static.
onboarding.s3_title: How to Build Your Global Strategy
onboarding.s3_intro: Each round, you make decisions across three strategic layers:
onboarding.s3_layer1: Design platforms, invest in R&D, choose which capabilities to build. Your technology is what you sell. Gen 1 gets you started. Gen 2 and Gen 3 unlock new markets and customer segments — but require sustained investment and prerequisite capability.
onboarding.s3_layer2: Price, promote, distribute, and produce for each product in each market. Match your offering to what local customers actually value — not what works at home.
onboarding.s3_layer3: Talent and organizational structure. Market entry modes and localization investment. Financial management and tax structure. ESG commitments and governance. Alliances and acquisitions. These are the decisions that compound over time — get them right early and they pay dividends for 8 rounds.
onboarding.s3_read_first: Before you decide, READ. Your Strategic Briefing, Market Research, Competitive Intelligence, and Strategy Tools all exist to inform your choices. The teams that research before they act consistently outperform those that don't.
onboarding.s4_intro: Your Performance Index reflects how well you satisfy ALL stakeholders — not just customers.
onboarding.s4_customers: Do your products match what each market segment values? Is your pricing, promotion, and distribution aligned with local preferences?
onboarding.s4_investors: Three AI investment funds evaluate your strategy every round. Growth investors want R&D and expansion. Value investors want discipline and dividends. ESG investors want sustainability commitment. They trade your shares accordingly — and your share price affects your capital-raising ability.
onboarding.s4_regulators: Each government evaluates your local employment, compliance, ESG investment, and manufacturing presence. Score high and earn incentives. Score low and face restrictions.
onboarding.s4_partners: Your distribution effectiveness depends on local relationships. Invest in local commercial talent and distribution infrastructure.
onboarding.s4_coherence: Beyond stakeholder satisfaction, your STRATEGIC COHERENCE is evaluated. Do your decisions reinforce each other? A premium product in mass retail with below-market talent and no ESG investment is incoherent — and the sim knows it.
onboarding.s5_step1: Read your Dashboard and Financial Reports — understand what you have
onboarding.s5_step2: Study Market Research — learn what customers in your home market actually value
onboarding.s5_step3: Decide: optimize at home first, or start expanding immediately?
onboarding.s6_no_single_strategy: There's no single right strategy. But every winning strategy is coherent — every decision reinforces the others.
